entry.title: Behavioral Contracts for Human-Autonomy Teaming
entry.summary: Three research communities are independently converging on formalized behavioral agreements between humans and autonomous agents. This article bridges Human-Autonomy Teaming, AI alignment, and safety engineering, arguing that scoped operator-authored contracts are theoretically necessary and not merely convenient.

entry.title: Agentic AI in Regulated Infrastructure: Guardrails, Evidence, and the Accountability Gap
entry.summary: How to deploy autonomous AI agents in EU-regulated infrastructure without breaking your compliance posture. Covers guardrail architecture, evidence requirements under NIS2 and the AI Act, and the structural accountability gap that current tooling ignores.

entry.title: Reference Architecture: Maximum-Autonomy IT Operations
entry.summary: Reference architecture for maximising operational autonomy in IT infrastructure while maintaining compliance, safety, and auditability. Targets 70-85% automation of operational toil with explicit human decision gates.

entry.title: Enforceable Boundary Contracts for EU-Regulated Infrastructure
entry.summary: Why EU-regulated infrastructure must enforce governance constraints at runtime and produce signed evidence as a byproduct of normal operation. Covers NIS2, CRA, AI Act, Machinery Regulation, and boundary enforcement architecture.
